Coloc..Abdomen, hard: The pains in the lower part of the abdomen are relieved by drawing up the limbs and pressing with the fists.
Coloc..Back: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
Coloc..Back, pain: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
Coloc..Back, pain, dorsal region: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
Coloc..Extremities: Where the pain is better from hard pressure and from heat, where it is worse during repose and drives the patient to despair, Colocynth will generally cure.
Coloc..Generalities: The principal feature of Colocynth is its severe, tearing, neuralgic pains; so severe that the patient is unable to keep still. Sometimes they are > by motion - at least it appears that they are worse during rest- > by pressure and sometimes > by heat.
Sometimes they are > by motion - at least it appears that they are worse during rest- > by pressure and sometimes > by heat.
Coloc..Genitalia female: In the violent ovarian neuralgias of Colocynth, the woman will flex the limb of the painful side hard against the abdomen and hold it there.
Coloc..Genitalia female, pain: In the violent ovarian neuralgias of Colocynth, the woman will flex the limb of the painful side hard against the abdomen and hold it there.
Coloc..Head: Painful, tearing, digging through the whole brain, becoming unbearable when moving the eyelids. Intense pain through the whole head; worse from moving the eyes. Severe, pressing, tearing headache, causing her to cry out.
Coloc..Mind: As soon as the prover of Colocynth begins to have pains along the course of nerves he becomes irritable; everything vexes him; he is worse from vexation.
Coloc..Mind, irritability: As soon as the prover of Colocynth begins to have pains along the course of nerves he becomes irritable; everything vexes him; he is worse from vexation.
Coloc..Mind, restlessness, nervousness: Walks about the room and becomes increasingly anxious as the pain goes on.
Coloc..Rectum: Diarrhoea and dysentery from anger with indignation; the most awful tenesmus during stool; urging to stool with colic.
Coloc..Stomach: Aversion to food. Violent thirst. Colic brought on from drinking while overheated; from eating indigestible things, from high living; colic from eating potatoes.
Coloc..Stomach, pain: The stomach pains are clutching, cramping and digging; as if grasped by the fingers.
Coloc..Abdomen, pain: Similar pains occur lower down in the abdomen, but they are still better from hard pressure, and from doubling up which amounts to pressure, come on in paroxysms of increasing severity, until the patient is nauseated and vomits, and are associated with great restlessness and faint, sinking feeling at the pit of the stomach.
Coloc..Generalities, pain: The principal feature of Colocynth is its severe, tearing, neuralgic pains; so severe that the patient is unable to keep still.
Coloc..Generalities, pressure: All pains are better from pressure, but this is in the beginning. After the pain has been going for several days with increasing severity, the part becomes very sensitive and pressure cannot be endured.
Coloc..Generalities, side: They are generally on the left side; while those of Belladonna are on the right, and are caused by cold.
Coloc..Mind, restlessness: In spite of extreme restlessness there is great weakness with the pains.
